It belongs to the genus Unona, which is a group of plants characterized by their unique flowers and fruits. The Annonaceae family is a diverse group of plants, commonly known as the custard apple family, and includes over 2,000 species worldwide. intro: Unona ancana, also known as Grains of Paradise, is a tropical plant native to West Africa, belonging to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). The health benefits of Unona ancana include its use in traditional medicine to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost energy levels, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. Bioactive constituents of Unona ancana include gingerols, shogaols, and zingiberene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Unona ancana is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as being used to flavor foods and beverages, and is traditionally combined with other herbs to make a stimulating and digestive aid drink known as "Achicha" in Ghana.
